name: Financial Services and Markets Act 2023 (FSMA 2023)
summary: The Financial Services and Markets Act 2023 is the UK's post-Brexit overhaul of financial services regulation. It revokes retained EU financial services law, transfers regulatory powers to the FCA and PRA, introduces a new framework for crypto-asset regulation, and establishes secondary competitiveness and growth objectives for UK financial regulators.
keyRequirements: Revocation of retained EU financial services law and transfer of rulemaking to FCA and PRA
Designated Activities Regime (DAR) for regulating specific financial activities outside traditional authorization
Crypto-asset regulation framework bringing stablecoins and broader crypto activities under FCA oversight
Secondary objectives for FCA and PRA to facilitate international competitiveness and medium-term growth
Senior Managers and Certification Regime (SMCR) extension and accountability framework
Access to cash: requirements to maintain reasonable provision of cash access services
penalties: FCA and PRA enforcement powers including unlimited fines for market abuse and regulatory breaches. Criminal penalties for unauthorized financial activities up to 2 years imprisonment. Public censure, prohibition orders, and restitution orders.
